name: Cold Poached Salmon W/Mustard Lime Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 Center-cut salmon fillets; skinned; 7-to-8 oz. each
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour 3 cups water and 1 1/2 cups wine into each of 2 large skillets and bring to boil.
2. Turn off heat.
3. Season salmon with salt and white pepper.
4. Place 3 fillets in each skillet; let stand for 6 minutes.
5. Turn salmon over in liquid; let stand for 5 minutes.
6. Bring liquid in skillets just to simmer and cook until salmon is cooked through, about 30 seconds.
7. Using a slotted spatula, transfer salmon to a platter. Cover and refrigerate until cold, about 3 hours or up to 1 day.
8. Let salmon stand at room temperature for 1 hour before serving.
9. Stir sour cream, mustard, lime juice, honey, and lime peel in a bowl to blend. Cover; chill until cold, at least 1 hour. (Can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.)
10. Arrange salmon on plates. Drizzle with some sauce. Top with basil. Serve, passing remaining sauce separately.
